sound_type: sustained musical purring -- long continuous purring trill, 10-30+ seconds, harmonic-rich, dense chorus creates wall of musical sound
ecosystem: Pond. Shallow, still or slow-moving water -- ponds, lake margins, seasonal pools, beaver ponds. Terrestrial outside breeding season.
season: Spring and early summer. Breeding choruses peak April through June depending on elevation and latitude.

coordinates.description: Reference for pond habitat in the PNW, Washington. Western Toads breed in ponds and shallow water from sea level to subalpine.

taxonomy_note.description: BUFONIDAE -- NEW FAMILY (47th). Third Anura family in engine. True toads. Hylidae (86, 90), Ranidae (96), Bufonidae (97). Four Anura entries across three families.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Bufonidae from Latin bufo ('toad'). True toads -- dry warty skin, parotoid glands, walking gait, sustained trilling call.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: PNW representative: Western Toad (Anaxyrus boreas). Sea level to subalpine. Softer, higher-pitched trill than Eastern species.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Anura -- continuing. Fourth entry. Three families. Frogs and toads. Each family brings a distinct acoustic signature.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Amphibia -- four Anura entries (86, 90, 96, 97). Three families. The amphibian chorus grows.
taxonomy_note.family_position: 47th family at degree 97. NEW FAMILY. Third Anura family. True toads. Sustained trilling call.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 1000-3000 Hz (species-dependent). Western Toad higher-pitched than Eastern species.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: NOT a song. SUSTAINED TRILL -- a long, continuous purring note produced by rapid laryngeal muscle vibration and vocal sac resonance.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual trill: 10-30+ seconds. Chorus: continuous for hours during peak breeding.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single sustained trill. No note variation within a call. One long, continuous purring tone. Smooth and unbroken.
song_metrics.note_types: Sustained purring trill (breeding call). No alarm calls, no clicks, no percussive elements. Pure sustained tonal trill.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: No phrase structure. Individual trill is one sustained note. Brief pause. Repeat. Chorus creates continuous overlap -- no silence.
song_metrics.timbre: Musical. Purring. Harmonic-rich. Smooth and resonant. The vocal sac creates a warm, full-bodied tone. Not harsh, not sharp -- rounded and musical. The most melodious amphibian call in the engine.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Loud and persistent. Multiple males create a dense wall of sound. Audible hundreds of meters from pond. Dominates nighttime soundscape near breeding sites.
song_metrics.tempo: No tempo. Sustained continuous trill. Brief pauses between trills. Chorus is continuous.
song_metrics.context: BREEDING CHORUS. Males gather at ponds and call to attract females. Peak calling at dusk through night. Spring and early summer. Pond habitat.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Western Toad (Anaxyrus boreas) in PNW -- softer, higher-pitched. American Toad (Anaxyrus americanus) in eastern range -- louder, lower. Both sustained trills.
song_metrics.key_property: SUSTAINED MUSICAL PURRING -- the 97th acoustic element (species side). Long continuous purring trill. 10-30+ seconds. E=4. Harmonic-rich. Dense breeding chorus. NEW FAMILY Bufonidae (47th). Third Anura family. Pond habitat. Thirty-seventh consecutive original CSV.

physical_description.overall: Family-level entry. Adult toads: 50-130 mm body length depending on species. Stout body. Short legs. Dry, warty skin. Prominent parotoid glands behind eyes. Walking gait rather than leaping.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: For degree 97: (1) DRY WARTY SKIN -- distinguishes from smooth-skinned frogs. (2) PAROTOID GLANDS -- large, prominent glands behind each eye. (3) SHORT LEGS -- walks rather than leaps. (4) STOUT BODY -- round and robust.
physical_description.skin_detail: Dry, bumpy, warty skin covered in glands. Parotoid glands secrete bufotoxins as defensive chemicals. Coloration typically brown, olive, or gray with dark mottling. Western Toad has a pale dorsal stripe.
physical_description.dental_detail: No teeth (true toads lack maxillary teeth, unlike many frogs). Wide mouth for insect capture.
physical_description.field_id: Stout-bodied amphibian with dry, warty skin and prominent parotoid glands behind eyes. Walks rather than leaps. Found near ponds during breeding season, terrestrial otherwise. The sustained trilling call is diagnostic -- no other PNW amphibian produces such a long continuous trill.

nodes.title: Sustained Musical Purring -- 97th Acoustic Element (species side)
nodes.description: Long, continuous purring trill lasting 10-30+ seconds. Harmonic-rich. E=4. Dense breeding chorus creates wall of musical sound. Most sustained amphibian call in the engine.

nodes.title: Bufonidae Enters -- 47th Family (NEW) / Third Anura Family
nodes.description: True toads. Third frog/toad family: Hylidae (86, 90), Ranidae (96), Bufonidae (97). Four Anura entries. Each family brings a distinct acoustic signature.

nodes.title: Anura Acoustic Survey -- Four Entries, Four Signatures
nodes.description: Pacific Treefrog kreek (86), Spring Peeper peep (90), Wood Frog cluck (96), Toad trill (97). Four entries, three families, four distinct vocalizations. The order Anura as a complete acoustic survey.

nodes.title: Pond Breeding Chorus -- Dusk Through Night
nodes.description: Males congregate at breeding ponds. Sustained trills attract females. Dozens to hundreds of individuals. Chorus continuous for hours. Spring and early summer.

nodes.title: The Postal Service Contrast -- Organic Purring vs Digital Purring
nodes.description: Degree 97 pairs toad chorus with Postal Service electronic. Both sustained, layered textures at E=4. Biological trill and algorithmic tenderness. Organic purring and digital purring.

cross_references.relationship: ANURA CONTINUES! Back-to-back Anura entries. Wood Frog (96, Ranidae, clucking) and Toad Chorus (97, Bufonidae, purring trill). Different families, different acoustic characters. Both pond breeders. Both E=4.

CHORUS PARALLEL! Both produce choruses that dominate nighttime soundscapes. Spring Peeper peeps (high, sharp, repeated). Toad trills (sustained, purring, continuous). Same ecological role: the amphibian chorus.
ANURA LINEAGE. First Anura at degree 86 (Hylidae). Now three families. Treefrog kreek vs toad trill -- sharply different acoustic characters within the same order.
POND CONNECTION. Muskrat builds lodges in the same pond habitat where toads breed. Different ecological niches, shared habitat.
POND BUILDER! Beaver creates pond habitat through dam construction. Toad choruses occur in beaver-created ponds. Ecosystem engineer (61) creates habitat for amphibian chorus (97).
